reviews.review_description: The level's fun The game is fun The only problem with this game is a steering if you turn your hand too fast or barely sometimes turn it your hands come off the steering wheel cause you to crash you lose tracking until you red grab the steering wheel again so when you don't need to hear the term you try to turn your hands don't stay grip to the steering wheel it pops off that needs to be fixed the steering when you try to hurry up and turn and do a slide your hands come off the steering wheel and stop responding until you re-grave it you got to turn it reals kind of slow so it will hands won't come off and lose tracking on the steering wheel please fix

Love n it so far lol. Definitely need an update for a few lil things like the actual driving, it sucks at the moment, but I have a blast with the game. True gem to play for some good ol fashioned fun. For a game focused on driving though an update or something is required because it’s sub par right now. Just saying 🤷🤙

I really enjoyed this game. Id give it a 9/10 but gave 5 stars on the meta rating to help the devs. Just finished every stage plus the dlc. I felt like a real stuntman the whole game. My only complain is the jumping can be glitchy and even if your straight on you don’t land good or flip and the slowmo thru the jumps is annoying you can’t skip it. Atleast add the option to skip the slowmo during the jumps please. Then it would be a 10/10 for me.

The game starts in the opening for a late night talk show, and you’re tonight’s guest, StuntDouble Joe McFlash. The show’s host gives a brief description of your stunt-double greatness, then you play through your various movie stunt scenes. There’s 7 different themed movies to play through with 6-9 scenes per movie, as well as several DLC scenes and a scene creator. Personally I’ve played through 2 whole movies so far and some of the DLC, but I haven’t tried the scene creator yet.
The game is well made, and is really fun to play. The scenes become more difficult as you progress through the movies, and some will take multiple tries to complete. The plot of the game is really interesting, and the script and voice acting are top notch. The controls work well, and you can even use the left thumbstick to steer if hands on the steering wheel isn’t your thing. The game music fits nicely with the themes of the movies you are playing in. All in all it’s a really great stunt driving game for the price. I would recommend StuntDouble Joe McFlash to anyone that enjoys mission-based driving games.
I should mention that I received this game for free through a giveaway in a Facebook group that I’m a member of which is Meta Quest Promotions, Giveaways, and Referrals, but it doesn’t affect my review in any way. Thanks to the admins of that group for all they do getting the word out about all of these great VR games. Most importantly congratulations to Moonshine Games, the developer of StuntDouble Joe McFlash. It’s a great game!
The concept of the game is pretty good but still needs a little work. Controlling the cars isn’t the best and can be a little finicky but all in all a good game.

Step into the shoes of Hollywood's most celebrated stunt driver. It’s like been back in the 80’s and the game has that sort of feel.  The graphics are cartoony and simplistic but vibrant and look cool as you go through slow mo scenes as you do stunts during filming on TV/film sets.  It makes you feel like you're starring in your own high-octane action film, pulling off insane stunts and surviving impossible scenarios . Control took a while for me to get to grips with, but when I realised you can use the thumbstick it became a whole lot better and suited how I play racing games.  I have only just played enough to get a feel and leave a review for this early access game but I do look forward to getting more into it and hope the dev continues to develop and support it.

Really like this one, it’s unique gameplay, its presentation, graphic style and sound.

It’s really good how it starts off as a tv interview on a show, inserting tapes for each level.

I found myself retrying over and over… I must do it!! lol

Like how some parts are in slow motion and even slow motion replay.

If you fancy yourself as a bit of a Fall Guy, then this is one for you.

Crazy low price… well worth it!

It’s like actually being in a movie, o feel like I’m the star of the show.
Great variety of stunts, dev has worked hard and we should show some love to this game.
Loving the graphics old school style, hope to see more try this game out.

Stunt Double offers thrilling scenes: jumps, chases, and spectacular stunts.
Its concept of revisiting the great Hollywood stunt scenes is very interesting.
With a very successful talk-show intro, its variety of settings (snow, desert, cities) and different vehicles, it's very fun and varied.
The challenges are fast-paced, the stunts enjoyable, and you can really feel the care taken with the speed effects and animations. Controlling the cars is simple but requires precision, especially when cornering or jumping.
Not everything is perfect: some menus are unresponsive, the interface lacks polish, and the game feels a bit uneven depending on the missions (some are very fun, others less inspired).
For the price of €4, you'll have fun driving cars inspired by Batman, The A-Team, or even K2000's Kit!
The game offers a level editor mode for creating your own scenes. An original driving game with a narrative twist, Stunt Double is well worth a try.
Despite its few imperfections, it's fun, and that's the main thing!

I got the key from FB GA.
I thought I'd overcome motion sickness, but I haven't completely overcome it in a car drive yet. You'll have to do a stunt to complete scenes for each movies. I can't say that the graphics or sound effects are great, but I think you'll have enough fun with the increasingly difficult challenges and various stages.

just purchased this game Again and i must admit, massiveimprovement andupdates and car can be controlled now usind thumb stick not very steady yet but massive improvement on everything. will not ask for a refund cos the game is soo good. hoping for just alittle bit for updates and stability of the stunt car❤️❤️

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, have you tried making your own Stunt yet in the Creator mode?

reviews.review_description:
I got this game on the day it was released and I've not been disappointed! The graphics are simple, but the gameplay means it really doesn't matter. You're a stunt driver and you race through check points on various film sets. Some levels are much trickier than others, which is a good thing, as there's a sense of achievement when you complete them. When you jump obstacles you get to watch your car fly in slow motion, and this is surprisingly satisfying!

Initially I thought holding the steering wheel with 2 hands was a little awkward, but now the car can be controlled with the left thumbstick, which I always prefer in VR racing games. This is no ordinary racing game though, and there's nothing else like it in the Meta store. Even though this game is still in Early Access (so there's some minor bugs), it already feels like a proper game that's worthy of your money, and I highly recommend it!
